title: Vangueriella campylacantha
intro: Vangueriella campylacantha is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Africa and commonly referred to as the "bitter leaf" due to its bitter taste. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Vangueriella campylacantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are also used to treat various ailments, including malaria and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Vangueriella campylacantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Vangueriella, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical Africa. The Rubiaceae family, also known as the madder family, comprises over 13,000 species of plants. Vangueriella campylacantha is a shrub or small tree that is commonly found in woodland areas. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.
